title: The New South
description: Practice your skills in this exercise on the New South.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

>“Even if investors had seized quickly upon industrial opportunities, if risk were not a factor, if all hesitant planters had ignored their neighbor’s scorn, and if the socially conscious had considered only their private gains, the South might still have seemed under-industrialized to outside observers. The region’s factor endowment would still have led to an industrial sector that was small relative to that in the East.” 

>-Fred Bateman and Thomas Weiss, economists, *A Deplorable Scarcity,* 2002

**Which of the following is one important continuity in the South throughout the 1880s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The South had a primarily agricultural economy based on sharecropping and tenant farming.
Southern leaders industrialized the South, boosting the southern economy over the northern economy.
Sharecropping and tenant farming died out as farmers moved out West to live on government-provided land.
The practice of sharecropping led many African Americans to move  North to find better wages.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage and answer the question below. 

>“Even if investors had seized quickly upon industrial opportunities, if risk were not a factor, if all hesitant planters had ignored their neighbor’s scorn, and if the socially conscious had considered only their private gains, the South might still have seemed under-industrialized to outside observers. The region’s factor endowment would still have led to an industrial sector that was small relative to that in the East.” 

>-Fred Bateman and Thomas Weiss, economists, *A Deplorable Scarcity,* 2002

**The patterns described in the excerpt most directly foreshadowed which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the continued dominance of northern manufacturing
the mass migration to the West for available government land
the emergence of cooperative farming organizations
the gradual shift towards an agricultural southern economy

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“We have got to go to manufacturing to save ourselves. We have got to go to it to obtain an increase of population. Workmen go to furnaces, mines, and factories— they go where labor is brought. Every new furnace or factory is sure to come from the neighborhood or from abroad. . . . Capital, to the extent that the South shall have occasion to borrow, will, by law of economy that never fails, flow here to erect, equip and start every manufacturing establishment as fast as it can profitably be run.”

>-J.D.B. DeBow, *De Bow’s Review,* 1867

**The excerpt best serves as evidence of which of the following trends during the 1860s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: calls from southern leaders to industrialize the South to compete with northern industry
racial violence and discrimination against African Americans living in the South
new trade relationships with Japan and China that increased the sale of raw materials
development of labor unions and cooperative organizations fighting for increased government regulation

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“We have got to go to manufacturing to save ourselves. We have got to go to it to obtain an increase of population. Workmen go to furnaces, mines, and factories— they go where labor is brought. Every new furnace or factory is sure to come from the neighborhood or from abroad. . . . Capital, to the extent that the South shall have occasion to borrow, will, by law of economy that never fails, flow here to erect, equip and start every manufacturing establishment as fast as it can profitably be run.”

>-J.D.B. DeBow, *De Bow’s Review,* 1867

**The ideas discussed in the excerpt led to which of the following economic changes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: New factories emerged in the South and introduced manufacturing into the southern economy.
Cotton was no longer a  vital part of the southern economy, as workers sought high-paying factory jobs.
Factories opened up new earning  opportunities for the lower classes, ending widespread poverty in the South.
Race relations worsened in the South as Southern leaders called for white supremacy in the South.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Once segregation began, there was no logical place for it to stop. If railroad cars were segregated, why not railroad stations, even ticket windows? If jails were segregated, why not courtrooms, even the Bibles on which witnesses swore? . . . Whites touted segregation as a way to ensure social peace, to reduce conflict in public places, to make sure that blacks received at least some social services. The newer a place or institution, the more certain it was to be segregated.” 

>-Edward L. Ayers, historian, *The Promise of the New South: Life After Reconstruction,* 2007.

**The developments described in the excerpt best illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: suppression of African American civil rights after Reconstruction
persistence of discrimination against European immigrants in the United States
industrialization of the southern economy following the Civil War
increased violence against African Americans by white supremacists

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Once segregation began, there was no logical place for it to stop. If railroad cars were segregated, why not railroad stations, even ticket windows? If jails were segregated, why not courtrooms, even the Bibles on which witnesses swore? . . . Whites touted segregation as a way to ensure social peace, to reduce conflict in public places, to make sure that blacks received at least some social services. The newer a place or institution, the more certain it was to be segregated.” 

>-Edward L. Ayers, historian, *The Promise of the New South: Life After Reconstruction,* 2007.

**Which of the following most likely contributed to the rise of segregation described in the excerpt?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Supreme Court decision in *Plessy v. Ferguson* (1896)
the ratification of the Fourteenth Amendment
the passage of Black Codes across  southern states
the rise in race riots following the end of the Civil War

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Color line cartoon](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/82d26dc9d095178c1c9b37cf3cd6c91f.jpg)
*Editorial cartoon criticizing the usage of literacy tests for African Americans as a qualification to vote. The cartoon shows a man writing on wall, "Eddikashun qualifukashun. The Black man orter be eddikated afore he kin vote with us Wites [Education qualification. The Black man ought to be educated before he can vote with us whites], signed Mr. Solid South."* 

Source: “The color line still exists - in this case,” *Harper’s Weekly,* 1879. Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_color_line_still_exists%E2%80%94in_this_case_cph.3b29638.jpg)

**The ideas expressed through the image reveal that in 1879,  which of the following was most true of the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: African Americans lost many of the political rights they gained during Reconstruction.
The federal government sought to protect African Americans from discrimination in the South.
African Americans migrated North to escape the oppression they faced in the southern states.
The federal government banned the use of literacy tests in all state and national elections.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“Give us our rights which belong to us. Give us fair and living wages for a fair day’s work. Treat our women with the respect due to their sex. Open the doors of lucrative business to our young men and women. Assist and encourage us to educate our children and bring them up in refinement whenever our means will admit of it.” 

>-“An Appeal to the White People of the South,” *Virginia Star,* 1882

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased discrimination against African Americans
passage of a law that limited immigration from China
rise of labor unions to fight against unsafe working conditions
surge of immigrant participation in the Democratic Party

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“Give us our rights which belong to us. Give us fair and living wages for a fair day’s work. Treat our women with the respect due to their sex. Open the doors of lucrative business to our young men and women. Assist and encourage us to educate our children and bring them up in refinement whenever our means will admit of it.” 

>-“An Appeal to the White People of the South,” *Virginia Star,* 1882

**The excerpt best serves as evidence of which of the following developments during the 1880s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ongoing demands for political and social equality for African Americans
growing pressure by labor unions to improve working conditions
increased conflict between European immigrants and African Americans
efforts to reduce immigration to the United States from Asian countries
